description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a coarse, uneven surface. Its coloration is predominantly dark gray with subtle variations, including lighter gray and hints of brown, giving it a natural, weathered appearance. The rock's surface is speckled and mottled, with visible mineral grains and rough textural details that suggest it is composed of dense stone, possibly granite or basalt. One side of the rock features a small, faint marking or number, indicating possible cataloging or identification. The overall mass and density of the rock make it a substantial obstacle, suitable for use as a barrier or natural landscaping element in an urban environment. Its form is solid and compact, with no visible cavities or openings.
description: A dark gray, rugged rock with an irregular, angular shape and coarse, mottled surface, featuring subtle mineral variations and a faint marking on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's most angular face, with a visible marking or number on the left side and pronounced surface texture.
description_view_1: This side reveals a broader, flatter surface with less visible marking, highlighting the mottled gray coloration and rough texture.
description_view_2: The rock appears more triangular from this angle, with a prominent peak and a dense, grainy surface.
description_view_3: This view displays a relatively flat, wide face with subtle color variations and a coarse, granular texture.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
